description_long: This object is a large, flat, rectangular structure with a corrugated metallic surface, featuring several mounted solar panel arrays. The solar panels are dark, grid-patterned, and are positioned at different angles on the surface, suggesting optimization for sunlight capture. The main body appears to be a roof or platform, likely made of metal, with the solar panels supported by robust frames. The panels are distributed in a non-uniform arrangement, with some mounted flat and others tilted. The structure is industrial in appearance, suitable for installation on commercial or utility buildings. No visible cabling or inverter units are apparent, and the surface is mostly smooth with some textural variation from the corrugation.
description: A large, corrugated metal platform with several mounted solar panel arrays, some flat and some tilted, designed for solar energy capture.
description_view_0: From this view, the platform's corrugated surface and two main solar panel arrays—one large and one small—are clearly visible, both mounted at different positions.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the tilt of the solar panels, showing one panel angled upward and another flat, emphasizing the mounting structure.
description_view_2: The opposite end view shows the platform's length and the arrangement of the panels, with the corrugated texture prominent and fewer panels visible.
description_view_3: This side view displays both tilted solar panels at opposite ends of the platform, revealing their angular orientation and the supporting frames.
attribute_panel_orientation: Panels are both flat and tilted at approximately 30 degrees, with tilt direction varying; orientation appears fixed.
attribute_mounting_structure_type: roof-mounted with visible metal frames, likely aluminum or steel.
attribute_array_configuration: Panels are arranged in a non-uniform cluster, with at least three distinct panel groups spaced across the platform.
